title: Earnings Call Transcript: TeraWulf Q1 2026 EPS Misses, Stock Volatile
text: TeraWulf held its Q1 2026 earnings call following a significant EPS miss. The company reported a loss of $0.44 per share, substantially worse than the consensus estimate of $0.16 loss. CEO Paul Prager and the management team discussed the company's strategic transition from Bitcoin mining to AI and HPC infrastructure, citing the newly established platform and initial operating performance. Management highlighted the $3.1 billion cash position, which provides substantial liquidity for continued expansion. The company reiterated its growth strategy targeting 250-500 MW of new contracted capacity annually. Discussion focused on the Lake Mariner facility's progress toward 440 MW of contracted capacity and the Abernathy Texas joint venture targeting delivery in Q4 2026. Despite the earnings miss, management expressed confidence in the longer-term revenue stability provided by contracted HPC leasing agreements backed by investment-grade counterparties.
